MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom:
Jason M. Rose, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing for Plaintiffs;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: Defendant Fairfax Building Supply Co., Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication.

No appearance by the moving party at the call of the matter. Mr. Rose stated that they did not receive any contests. No argument is held. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Fairfax Building Supply Co., Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication is OFF CALENDAR. Opposition filed.

Plaintiffs' complaint alleges that Stanford Hardman's ("Decedent") mesothelioma was a result of his exposure to asbestos products which were manufactured, supplied, disturbed, used, or distributed by Defendants, including Defendant Fairfax Building Supply Co., Inc ("Defendant") when he conducted home renovations.

Plaintiffs argue that Defendant's motion is fatally defective because they did not receive sufficient notice of the motion as required under Code of Civil Procedure sections 437c(a) [pre- January 1, 2025 amendment] and 1010.6(a)(4)(B).) Plaintiffs contend that Defendant's motion is untimely served because Defendant failed to provide Plaintiffs with the requisite two additional court days for e-service. The Court agrees.

Code of Civil Procedure Section 437c(a)(2) provides that "notice of the motion and supporting papers shall be served on all other parties to the action at least 75 days before the time appointed for hearing." If the notice is served electronically, the required 75-day period of notice must be increased by two court days. (Code Civ. Proc., Sections 437c(a)(2) and 1010.6(a)(4)(B); see Cole v. Superior Court (2022) 87 Cal.App.5th 84, 87-88.) The Court cannot shorten the notice period without the parties' prior consent. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 764; McMahon v. Super. Ct. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 117-118.) The Court cannot cure the defect by continuing the hearing for the missing number of days. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-1268.)

Here, Defendant's proof of service reflects that Defendant delivered its notice of motion by electronic service and filing on Thursday, December 5, 2024. However, the last day for such method of delivery for hearing on February 18, 2025 was Tuesday, December 3, 2024.

Based on the foregoing, the Court agrees with Plaintiffs that the notice of the motion was not timely served. Furthermore, the Court cannot cure the defect by continuing the motion to a later hearing date. Accordingly, Defendant's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar.

Court to review the proposed order submitted by counsel for Plaintiff.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (304/EPS)
